verses.text: Paul and Timothy to the saints at Philippi, with the bishops and deacons; grace and peace. Warm greeting. He writes with deep affection to this church.
Paul and Timothy to the saints at Philippi, with the bishops and deacons; grace and peace. Warm greeting. He writes with deep affection to this church.
I thank God for you, confident 'that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it.' Sure work. What God starts in grace He will complete.
I thank God for you, confident 'that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it.' Sure work. What God starts in grace He will complete.
I thank God for you, confident 'that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it.' Sure work. What God starts in grace He will complete.
I thank God for you, confident 'that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it.' Sure work. What God starts in grace He will complete.
I have you in my heart; God is my record how greatly I long after you. Deep love. His care for them is tender and true.
I have you in my heart; God is my record how greatly I long after you. Deep love. His care for them is tender and true.
I pray your love abound 'in knowledge,' filled with the fruits of righteousness. Growing love. Love is to deepen in wisdom and holy fruit.
I pray your love abound 'in knowledge,' filled with the fruits of righteousness. Growing love. Love is to deepen in wisdom and holy fruit.
I pray your love abound 'in knowledge,' filled with the fruits of righteousness. Growing love. Love is to deepen in wisdom and holy fruit.
My bonds have furthered the gospel; many wax bold to speak the word. Good from chains. His imprisonment only spread the gospel.
My bonds have furthered the gospel; many wax bold to speak the word. Good from chains. His imprisonment only spread the gospel.
My bonds have furthered the gospel; many wax bold to speak the word. Good from chains. His imprisonment only spread the gospel.
Some preach Christ of envy, some of love; whether in pretence or truth, 'Christ is preached; and I... do rejoice.' Christ proclaimed. He rejoices that Christ is named, whatever the motive.
Some preach Christ of envy, some of love; whether in pretence or truth, 'Christ is preached; and I... do rejoice.' Christ proclaimed. He rejoices that Christ is named, whatever the motive.
Some preach Christ of envy, some of love; whether in pretence or truth, 'Christ is preached; and I... do rejoice.' Christ proclaimed. He rejoices that Christ is named, whatever the motive.
Some preach Christ of envy, some of love; whether in pretence or truth, 'Christ is preached; and I... do rejoice.' Christ proclaimed. He rejoices that Christ is named, whatever the motive.
This shall turn to my salvation; Christ shall 'be magnified in my body... by life, or by death.' Magnify Christ. His one aim is to honour Christ either way.
This shall turn to my salvation; Christ shall 'be magnified in my body... by life, or by death.' Magnify Christ. His one aim is to honour Christ either way.
'For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain'; to depart and be with Christ is far better. Life or gain. Christ is his whole life and his hoped-for reward.
'For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain'; to depart and be with Christ is far better. Life or gain. Christ is his whole life and his hoped-for reward.
'For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain'; to depart and be with Christ is far better. Life or gain. Christ is his whole life and his hoped-for reward.
'For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain'; to depart and be with Christ is far better. Life or gain. Christ is his whole life and his hoped-for reward.
Yet I shall abide for your furtherance and joy of faith. Staying for them. He is content to remain for their good.
Yet I shall abide for your furtherance and joy of faith. Staying for them. He is content to remain for their good.
Let your conversation be 'as becometh the gospel'; it is given you to suffer for His sake. Worthy walk. Suffering for Christ is itself a gift.
